sections.title: Limit Visualizers
sections.subtitle: Interactive tools for limits and continuity — more coming soon.

visualizers.title: Fake Proof: π = 4
visualizers.description: Refine a staircase around a circle and watch the shape converge — while the perimeter stubbornly stays at 4. A classic limits cautionary tale.

sections.title: Derivative Visualizers
sections.subtitle: See rates of change, secant lines, and Taylor approximations in motion.

visualizers.title: Secant to Tangent
visualizers.description: Slide the second point along the curve and watch the secant line approach the tangent — the geometric heart of the derivative.

visualizers.title: Taylor Polynomial Approximations
visualizers.description: Build Taylor polynomials of increasing degree around a point and see how each added term improves the fit to sin(x).

sections.title: Integral Visualizers
sections.subtitle: Explore Riemann sums, area functions, and the Fundamental Theorem.

visualizers.title: Riemann Sum Explorer
visualizers.description: Adjust the interval, number of rectangles, and sample point to see how Riemann sums approximate the area under a curve.

visualizers.title: Fundamental Theorem of Calculus, Part 2
visualizers.description: Trace the area function F(x) = ∫ₐˣ f(t) dt and discover why its derivative recovers the integrand — FTC Part 2 in motion.
